2605.04874.title: Uncertainty-Aware Exploratory Direct Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models
2605.04874.paper_summary: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) has proven to be an effective solution for mitigating hallucination in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) by learning from preference pairs. One of its key challenges lies in how to transfer the sequence-level preference into fine-grained supervision on visual fidelity. To safeguard vision-related tokens that are prone to hallucination, existing methods typically allocate training emphasis according to the model's self-assessed visual sensitivity signals. However, such sensitivity, estimated by a model still under training, introduces self-referential bias: reinforcing already well-learned visual cues while neglecting hard-to-perceive but critical details, thereby limiting deeper alignment. In this work, we propose an Uncertainty-aware Exploratory Direct Preference Optimization (UE-DPO) method for MLLMs, which enables the model to uncover its cognitive deficiencies and actively explore for self-correction, guided by token-level epistemic uncertainty. Specifically, we first quantify the uncertainty from the model's failure to ground token predictions in the given image. Then, based on an uncertainty-aware exploration intensity, we encourage more learning pressure on visually deficient tokens in preferred samples, and alleviate the over-penalization of beneficial knowledge in dispreferred samples. Further, we provide a theoretical justification for our method, and extensive experiments demonstrate its effectiveness and robustness.
2605.04874.paper_summary_zh: 直接偏好優化（DPO）已被證明是一種有效的解決方案，可以通過從偏好對中學習來減輕多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）中的幻覺問題。它的一個主要挑戰在於如何將序列級別的偏好轉化為對視覺真實性的細緻監督。為了保護易於產生幻覺的與視覺相關的標記，現有的方法通常根據模型自我評估的視覺敏感信號來分配訓練重點。然而，這種敏感性是由仍在訓練中的模型估算的，這引入了自我參照偏差：強化已經學得良好的視覺線索，同時忽略難以感知但至關重要的細節，從而限制了更深層次的對齊。在本研究中，我們提出了一種不確定性感知的探索性直接偏好優化（UE-DPO）方法，適用於 MLLMs，該方法使模型能夠發現其認知缺陷並主動探索以進行自我修正，並以標記級別的認知不確定性為指導。具體來說，我們首先量化模型在給定圖像中無法對標記預測進行基準的情況下的不確定性。然後，基於不確定性感知的探索強度，我們鼓勵在偏好的樣本中對視覺不足的標記施加更多的學習壓力，並減輕對不受偏好的樣本中有益知識的過度懲罰。此外，我們為我們的方法提供了理論依據，並通過大量實驗證明了其有效性和穩健性。
2605.04874.author: Huatian Zhang et.al.
2605.04874.authors: Huatian Zhang, Zhendong Mao, Lei Zhang, Yongdong Zhang
